decision_summary: The chip max temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity, which is currently at 425.25 W/(m*K). I will increase it by a factor of 1.2 to 510.3, but this exceeds the upper bound of 500.0. Therefore, I will set it to the maximum allowed value of 500.0 W/(m*K) to maximize lateral heat spreading and reduce the peak temperature.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat diffusion away from the chip, directly lowering the peak temperature. This hits the defined upper bound for maximum effect.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur as conductivity approaches very high values relative to other materials
If this single change is insufficient, subsequent steps may need to adjust spreader geometry (width/height)
